summary: Phase 3 backend testing complete. All 62 backend tests pass (48 previous + 14 new notification tests). Verified Telegram/WhatsApp endpoints: status, settings (GET masked + defaults), settings (PUT persistence + empty 400 + admin-only), test (graceful no-op with reasons when creds missing, no 500), log endpoint, disable channel behavior. WHATSAPP_TO_DEFAULT correctly surfaces as 'whatsapp:+37125522773' from env. All previously passing flows (auth, products, cart, orders, discounts, admin chat, /chat, Stripe checkout/status) still pass.

critical.issue: Function body is DUPLICATED (two identical loops: first ends ~line 642, second is a stray docstring + identical block ~lines 644-679). For every paid order, supplier notifications are sent TWICE and TWO entries are inserted into supplier_notifications per supplier. Will cause double WhatsApp/Telegram messages once creds are added, and duplicate log entries today.

minor.issue: Logic skips empty strings (v != ""), which means admins cannot clear/unset a previously stored value through this endpoint (e.g., to wipe a wrong telegram_chat_id, they must update with a new value). Not blocking; surfaces only as UX limitation.
telegram_chat_id is returned UNMASKED (intended per problem statement — verified). twilio_whatsapp_from and whatsapp_to_default also returned plain (acceptable since they are not secrets).

action_items: Remove the duplicated second loop inside _notify_suppliers_for_order in /app/backend/server.py (delete lines 644-679 approx — the stray docstring + duplicated for-loop). Keep only the first block (lines 608-642).
Optional: allow clearing settings fields (e.g., pass null to unset) in PUT /api/notifications/settings.
critical_code_review_comments: DUPLICATE CODE BUG: _notify_suppliers_for_order contains the same logic twice. Each paid order will trigger duplicate Telegram + WhatsApp sends and create 2 supplier_notifications DB rows per supplier. Fix before going to production.

Notifications module is clean, graceful no-op pattern is correctly implemented (returns {sent: False, reason: ...} rather than raising). Good error handling, async/await usage correct, Twilio sync SDK correctly off-loaded via run_in_executor.
channel_status / settings endpoints properly admin-gated via Depends(require_admin); customer correctly receives 403, unauthenticated receives 401.
Secrets are masked on GET (last-4 only) which is good. Non-secret fields (chat_id, from/to numbers, enabled flags) returned plain as expected.
seed_data_creation: Test customers created with TEST_ email prefix during tests; notification_settings DB cleaned post-run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 14 notification tests live in TestNotifications class in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. After main agent fixes the duplicate _notify_suppliers_for_order block, re-run pytest and additionally verify supplier_notifications collection only gets ONE entry per supplier per paid order (currently 2). Notification settings persist in db.notification_settings doc id='global'.
rca of the issue: The duplicate code in _notify_suppliers_for_order looks like an accidental copy-paste — there is a stray triple-quoted docstring at line 644 followed by an identical for-loop. Python treats the docstring as a no-op string expression and then proceeds to execute the duplicated loop, doubling all notification work. Reproduction: place a paid order (simulated via Stripe webhook or status check), then query db.supplier_notifications — you'll see 2 rows per supplier. Mitigation: delete lines ~643-679 (everything after the first loop's await db.supplier_notifications.insert_one(...) block).
